behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies: a unit test of one parser function can miss resolver/lockfile wiring issues (especially transitive graph expansion). No real network/OS boundary is required, so System is not minimal.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner dependency-discovery against fixture projects on disk (e.g., package.json+lockfile, requirements.txt+lock data), then assert the produced dependency list sent to the query layer contains expected direct and transitive packages with versions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE apply: request-shape/parameterization bugs often only surface against the real API contract. A mock server can accidentally accept name-only queries and hide real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vuln DB endpoint (or official staging), submit manifests where package name is constant but version ranges differ, and assert returned CVEs differ appropriately by range and that scanner request includes precise version constraints.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic ordering logic; failures are reproducible by direct inputs/outputs with no I/O or external dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call sort routine with mixed severities and equal-severity CVEs with varied CVSS values; assert exact ordered list: severity rank first, CVSS descending as tiebreaker.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies: single-function tests can miss parser/serializer round-trip effects (reordering, normalization, accidental edits) across modules and file handling.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end manifest update on real fixture files, then diff before/after and assert only one dependency version token changed and all other dependency entries are byte-for-byte unchanged (or canonicalized per explicit formatter contract).

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: this crosses subprocess/git hosting API boundaries. Mocked PR clients can hide auth/branch/diff wiring failures.
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repository, run scanner autofix path and verify an actual PR is opened with exactly one dependency bump in changed files and no unrelated modifications.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Deterministic semver rule evaluation with direct input tuples; no external boundary required.
behaviors.test_description: Test comparator with representative version pairs (e.g., 1.x->2.x breaking, 1.2->1.3 non-breaking, pre-release edge cases) and assert breaking/non-breaking classification.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies at orchestration boundary: need semver detector + decision engine + reporting wired together. Real external PR API is not minimal if a spy/fake adapter can prove no create call and output flag propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner pipeline with an input that triggers breaking bump; assert orchestrator emits review-required flag in final report and PR adapter create method is not invoked.
